---
title: "Chapter 18: Using the Internet"
description: "Detailed study notes for Chapter 18 of Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 18
source_pages: "519-537"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch18-chapter-18-using-the-internet:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Chapter 18</span><span>Source pages 519-537</span></div>

This note summarises Chapter 18, "Using the Internet," from Kathryn E. Newcomer, Harry P. Hatry, and
Joseph S. Wholey's *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation*
[@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note
based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the chapter.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the chapter's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook tables, checklists, figures, boxes, or extended passages.

## Core Argument

Internet tools can support evaluation data collection and communication, but they require attention to coverage, access, privacy, usability, and data integrity.

## Study Summary

The chapter reviews web-enabled options for collecting, managing, and communicating evaluation
information.

It positions online methods as useful when they fit respondents, questions, and data protection
requirements.

The main risk is choosing online methods for convenience while excluding participants or weakening
data quality.

Within the wider book, this chapter belongs to Part 2: Practical Data Collection Procedures. It
should be read alongside the neighboring chapters because the handbook treats design, data
collection, analysis, and use as connected evaluation work.

## Key Concepts

- **internet data collection**: Online or web-enabled data collection, recruitment, communication, or observation for evaluation.
- **survey**: A structured data collection method used to obtain comparable responses from a population or sample.
- **data quality**: The completeness, accuracy, consistency, accessibility, and interpretability of data for evaluation purposes.

## Chapter Structure

- Design problem: Internet tools can support evaluation data collection and communication, but they require attention to coverage, access, privacy, usability, and data integrity.
- Evidence focus: The chapter reviews web-enabled options for collecting, managing, and communicating evaluation information.
- Practice method: It positions online methods as useful when they fit respondents, questions, and data protection requirements.
- Quality risk: The main risk is choosing online methods for convenience while excluding participants or weakening data quality.

## Practical Implications

- Check digital access and coverage before using online methods.
- Protect privacy and data security.
- Match online tools to the evaluation question and population.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Chapter 17: Collecting Data in the Field](chapter-17-collecting-data-in-the-field.qmd)
- Next: [Chapter 19: Semi-Structured Interviews](chapter-19-semi-structured-interviews.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Evaluation Planning and Design](section-01-planning-and-design.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations](section-02-planning-useful-evaluations.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation](section-03-performance-measurement.qmd)

## Study Prompts

- What decision or design problem does Chapter 18 help resolve?
- Which evidence source should be reopened before using this summary in applied work?
- Which quality risk in the chapter is most likely to affect a live evaluation?

## Source Records

- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch18-chapter-18-using-the-internet:c0001`
- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001`

## References
